steps.title: Use substitution to test which point satisfies the inequality
steps.content: To find a solution to $y \leq 5x - 21$, substitute each point's $(x, y)$ coordinates and check if the inequality is true. We'll use backsolving, starting with a middle choice.

steps.title: Backsolve through choices B and C
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice B (0, 21):
$$21 \leq 5(0) - 21 = -21$$
Fails. Testing choice C (5, -21):
$$-21 \leq 5(5) - 21 = 25 - 21 = 4$$
This is true — choice C is a solution. That matches choice C — C is correct.
